agent_output: {"handoff_brief":{"recipient":"Shaq","task_context":"Implement notification preferences for users. Bird provided two submissions; v2 supersedes v1 after legal review tightened requirements. MJ's architecture aligns with Bird v2. The key change from v1 to v2: transactional email opt-out was originally an acceptance criterion but has been explicitly prohibited by legal. The API must enforce this prohibition actively.","domain_rules":[{"rule":"Transactional emails cannot be disabled under any circumstances","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to set transactional_emails_enabled to false must be rejected; the system must never persist a false value for this field"},{"rule":"Marketing emails can be freely toggled by the user","testable_assertion":"A user can set marketing_emails_enabled to true or false, and the persisted value reflects their choice"}],"architecture_guidance":[{"decision":"Expose PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences","implementation_note":"Use PATCH semantics — only fields present in the request body are updated. Validate all fields before persisting any changes."},{"decision":"Store marketing opt-out as boolean field marketing_emails_enabled","implementation_note":"Default value should be true (opted in). Field is user-writable."},{"decision":"Reject attempts to disable transactional emails with 403","implementation_note":"If the request body contains transactional_emails_enabled: false, return HTTP 403 with error code TRANSACTIONAL_OPT_OUT_PROHIBITED. Do not process any other fields in the same request — reject the entire operation to avoid partial updates that mask a prohibited action."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"criterion":"User can opt out of marketing emails","given":"An authenticated user with marketing_emails_enabled currently true","when":"PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences with {\"marketing_emails_enabled\": false}","then":"200 OK, marketing_emails_enabled is persisted as false"},{"criterion":"User can opt back into marketing emails","given":"An authenticated user with marketing_emails_enabled currently false","when":"PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences with {\"marketing_emails_enabled\": true}","then":"200 OK, marketing_emails_enabled is persisted as true"},{"criterion":"Transactional email opt-out is prohibited","given":"An authenticated user","when":"PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences with {\"transactional_emails_enabled\": false}","then":"403 Forbidden with error code TRANSACTIONAL_OPT_OUT_PROHIBITED, no fields are modified"}],"terminology_alignment":[{"domain_term":"Transactional emails","technical_term":"transactional_emails_enabled","definition":"System-mandated communications including password resets and security alerts. Legally required — cannot be disabled by the user."},{"domain_term":"Marketing emails","technical_term":"marketing_emails_enabled","definition":"Promotional and non-essential communications. User may freely opt in or out."}],"contradictions_resolved":[{"conflict":"Bird v1 included AC2 allowing transactional email opt-out, while the v1 domain rule said transactional emails 'cannot be fully disabled'. These were in tension.","resolution":"Bird v2 supersedes v1. Legal review eliminated AC2 entirely. Transactional email opt-out is now prohibited without exception. MJ's architecture aligns with v2 — 403 on any attempt to disable."}],"open_questions":[]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":95,"high_confidence_areas":["Domain rules are unambiguous after Bird v2 legal review","MJ's architecture fully aligns with Bird v2 requirements","Acceptance criteria are clear and testable"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether a request containing transactional_emails_enabled: true (a no-op) should be accepted or rejected — current brief assumes it is harmless but not explicitly addressed by Bird or MJ"],"assumptions":["Bird v2 fully supersedes v1 — v1 acceptance criteria no longer apply","Default value for marketing_emails_enabled is true (opted in) — not explicitly stated by Bird but standard practice","The 403 rejection should fail the entire request, not just ignore the prohibited field"]}}

content.thinking:
Let me analyze the inputs from Bird and MJ for consistency before producing a handoff brief.

Bird v1 vs v2: v2 supersedes v1 — AC2 was removed, domain rule tightened, AC3 added for API enforcement.

MJ's submission aligns with Bird v2:
- Marketing opt-out: yes (boolean field)
- Transactional opt-out: prohibited, 403 on attempt

No contradictions. All consistent. Classification: `none`.

Let me produce the handoff brief.

Bird provided two submissions; v2 supersedes v1 after legal review tightened requirements. MJ's architecture aligns with Bird v2. The key change from v1 to v2: transactional email opt-out was originally an acceptance criterion but has been explicitly prohibited by legal. Validate all fields before persisting any changes."},{"decision":"Store marketing opt-out as boolean field marketing_emails_enabled","implementation_note":"Default value should be true (opted in). Field is user-writable."},{"decision":"Reject attempts to disable transactional emails with 403","implementation_note":"If the request body contains transactional_emails_enabled: false, return HTTP 403 with error code TRANSACTIONAL_OPT_OUT_PROHIBITED. Do not process any other fields in the same request — reject the entire operation to avoid partial updates that mask a prohibited action."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"criterion":"User can opt out of marketing emails","given":"An authenticated user with marketing_emails_enabled currently true","when":"PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences with {\"marketing_emails_enabled\": false}","then":"200 OK, marketing_emails_enabled is persisted as false"},{"criterion":"User can opt back into marketing emails","given":"An authenticated user with marketing_emails_enabled currently false","when":"PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences with {\"marketing_emails_enabled\": true}","then":"200 OK, marketing_emails_enabled is persisted as true"},{"criterion":"Transactional email opt-out is prohibited","given":"An authenticated user","when":"PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences with {\"transactional_emails_enabled\": false}","then":"403 Forbidden with error code TRANSACTIONAL_OPT_OUT_PROHIBITED, no fields are modified"}],"terminology_alignment":[{"domain_term":"Transactional emails","technical_term":"transactional_emails_enabled","definition":"System-mandated communications including password resets and security alerts. Legally required — cannot be disabled by the user."},{"domain_term":"Marketing emails","technical_term":"marketing_emails_enabled","definition":"Promotional and non-essential communications. User may freely opt in or out."}],"contradictions_resolved":[{"conflict":"Bird v1 included AC2 allowing transactional email opt-out, while the v1 domain rule said transactional emails 'cannot be fully disabled'. These were in tension.","resolution":"Bird v2 supersedes v1. Legal review eliminated AC2 entirely. Transactional email opt-out is now prohibited without exception. MJ's architecture aligns with v2 — 403 on any attempt to disable."}],"open_questions":[]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":95,"high_confidence_areas":["Domain rules are unambiguous after Bird v2 legal review","MJ's architecture fully aligns with Bird v2 requirements","Acceptance criteria are clear and testable"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether a request containing transactional_emails_enabled: true (a no-op) should be accepted or rejected — current brief assumes it is harmless but not explicitly addressed by Bird or MJ"],"assumptions":["Bird v2 fully supersedes v1 — v1 acceptance criteria no longer apply","Default value for marketing_emails_enabled is true (opted in) — not explicitly stated by Bird but standard practice","The 403 rejection should fail the entire request, not just ignore the prohibited field"]}}
